entry.title: Re: Need to disable multiple users
entry.content:
I do assume the following FAQ from this forum will be of help:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/how-to-temporarily-disable-connections-to-sa12-for-non-dba-users/qaq-p/13822667

entry.title: Re: 🇺🇸 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Houston, Texas, USA)
entry.content: Thank you to Thomas and Michelle for a successful CodeJam delivery in Sierra AppHaus Houstonhttps://www.linkedin.com/posts/carlos-estala_sierradigital-sapapphaus-sapapphausnetwork-activity-7308897132835454976-pJTr/?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAABssS08BpNqtg5xbzuprQFNRzTQJySaRUIQ

entry.title: Generative AI with SAP – Introduction to SAP Business AI
entry.content:
In today’s fast-evolving digital economy, businesses are increasingly seeking ways to enhance productivity, improve decision-making, and deliver personalized experiences. Artificial Intelligence (AI) has emerged as a cornerstone in this transformation, and SAP is at the forefront, embedding AI directly into its business processes through SAP Business AI.

This blog serves as an introduction to SAP Business AI, with a focus on Generative AI and its integration into the SAP ecosystem.

 

What Is SAP Business AI?

SAP Business AI is SAP’s portfolio of AI technologies and services embedded across its applications and platforms. Unlike generic AI platforms, SAP Business AI is enterprise-ready, context-aware, and responsible by design, tailored to help businesses become more intelligent and agile.

Key components of SAP Business AI include:

1.Embedded AI capabilities in SAP applications (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Customer Experience)

2.Industry-specific AI scenario

  1. Business-centric AI models trained on anonymized and consented SAP data
  2. Tools for developing, deploying, and managing AI

 

What Is Generative AI?

Generative AI refers to AI systems capable of generating new content, such as text, images, code, and even designs. It leverages large language models (LLMs) and transformers to understand and produce human-like outputs based on prompts.

In a business context, Generative AI opens up possibilities for:

  1. Automating document creation (e.g., invoices, job descriptions)
  2. Summarizing reports and meeting notes
  3. Writing personalized marketing content
  4. Assisting in coding and debugging
  5. Enhancing customer service with intelligent chatbots

 

Generative AI in SAP Business AI

SAP integrates Generative AI within its solutions through a responsible and enterprise-focused approach.

  1. SAP Joule – Your AI Copilot

SAP introduced Joule; a natural language copilot embedded across SAP cloud applications. Joule helps users:

  1. Retrieve business insights in real time
  2. Automate tasks with simple prompts
  3. Interact conversationally with enterprise data

For example, a procurement officer can ask:
What are the top 5 vendors based on last quarter’s delivery performance?
Joule delivers a contextual, data-driven answer instantly.

  1. Partnering with Leading LLM Providers

SAP collaborates with OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google Cloud Vertex AI to integrate advanced LLMs into its applications, while maintaining enterprise-grade data privacy and security.

  1. SAP AI Core and AI Launchpad

For developers, SAP AI Core provides infrastructure to manage and deploy AI models, including Generative AI. SAP AI Launchpad offers tools to monitor and govern these models responsibly.

 

Use Cases of Generative AI in SAP

Here are some real-world examples of how Generative AI is used across SAP solutions:

AreaUse Case
HR (SuccessFactors)Auto-generate job descriptions based on role requirements
Finance (S/4HANA)Summarize financial reports and detect anomalies
Customer ServiceGenerate dynamic responses in chatbots
ProcurementDraft vendor emails and contracts
Sales & MarketingCreate personalized campaign content

Responsible AI with SAP

SAP emphasizes ethical AI use, focusing on:

  1. Transparency: Clearly informing users when AI is used
  2. Privacy: Ensuring data is handled securely and anonymized
  3. Fairness: Avoiding bias in model training and outputs

With its AI Ethics Policy and governance frameworks, SAP is committed to delivering AI that customers can trust.

 

Getting Started

To explore SAP Business AI:

  1. Try out Joule in your SAP applications
  2. Explore the SAP AI Core and Launchpad on SAP BTP
  3. Visit the SAP Discovery Center for Generative AI use cases
  4. Join the SAP Community to connect with AI experts and peers

Generative AI is more than a buzzword - it’s a powerful tool driving intelligent automation and innovation across industries. With SAP Business AI, organizations can unlock the value of AI within familiar business processes, responsibly and securely.

Stay tuned for deep dives into specific use cases, technical integrations, and customer success stories in future posts.

 

What Is Joule?

Imagine having a trusted AI companion by your side, ready to assist and enhance your daily operations using the power of natural language and generative capabilities across SAP’s cloud portfolio that’s Joule.

Joule is:

  1. An advanced, generative AI copilot embedded throughout SAP’s cloud enterprise portfolio
  2. Designed to deliver proactive and contextualized insights from SAP applications and third-party sources
  3. Built to help people get work done faster and drive better business outcomes in a secure, compliant way

 

How Will Customers Use Joule?

Joule quickly sorts through and contextualizes data from multiple systems to surface smarter insights. It will be embedded across applications including HR, finance, supply chain, procurement, and customer experience, and integrated into SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

Users can ask questions or present problems in plain language and receive intelligent, context-aware answers.

For example:

  1. A manufacturer asks Joule about sales performance.
  2. Joule identifies underperforming regions, links it to supply chain data, and suggests fixes.
  3. In HR, it can write inclusive job descriptions and suggest interview questions.

 

Some Important Facts About Joule

Integration with SAP Applications:
Joule provides a conversational UI integrated with SAP apps, using SAP Fiori-compliant components.

Enterprise-Readiness:
Out-of-the-box integration with SAP backend systems, aligned with AI ethics, GDPR, and privacy controls. Joule is SOC-II compliant.

Automatic Updates:
Joule is continuously updated to include new capabilities and improvements.

 

How to Stay Updated on Joule

Explore these resources:

  1. SAP Joule product page
  2. SAP Community for Joule updates
  3. SAP Discovery Center for use cases

 

Joule in Procurement – Empowering Smart, AI-Driven Decisions

Procurement is a strategic function where speed, accuracy, and insight are critical. Joule brings transformative capabilities to procurement by enabling:

  1. Conversational access to enterprise data
  2. Automation of repetitive tasks
  3. Enhanced decision-making

Real-Time Spend Analysis

Instead of digging through reports, simply ask -
"What was our total spend on IT hardware in Q1 across all regions?"
Joule retrieves and visualizes the data instantly.

Supplier Evaluation

Ask:
Which suppliers had the highest late delivery rate last quarter?
Joule evaluates performance using embedded analytics and ML.

RFQ and Email Drafting

Joule can:

  1. Draft RFQs
  2. Create follow-up emails
  3. Propose contract language

Example:
Draft an RFQ for 500 ergonomic office chairs with delivery in two weeks.

Workflow Acceleration

Speed up approvals:

  1. Show PRs needing my approval
  2. Approve all low-risk PRs below $5,000

Compliance & Risk Detection

Stay audit-ready:
Which POs are missing documentation?
Joule flags issues proactively.

 

Why It Matters

In procurement, Joule enables:

  1. Faster decision-making
  2. Improved collaboration
  3. Proactive risk mitigation
  4. Higher operational efficiency

Joule doesn’t replace professionals — it amplifies them.

Coming soon: Expanded capabilities across the entire procurement lifecycle — from sourcing to supplier collaboration — all within the trusted SAP ecosystem.

 

https://www.sap.com/products/artificial-intelligence/ai-assistant.html

 

Thankyou...

entry.title: Re: How to use both Supplier Field mapping fields one is related to network and other is legal one?
entry.content:
Cannot make it work. Expecting data to flow from SBN to SLP questionnaires. cf https://help.sap.com/docs/strategic-sourcing/supplier-management-setup-and-administration/mapping-questions-to-sap-business-network-supplier-profile-fields-93bef317860a4a959c720e0ae8c18146?locale=en-US

Questions are set with answer type  as Text (single line) or Text (single line limited). Any advice?

entry.title: SAP Build Apps - Trying to Generate code with help of Joule to import excel file
entry.content:
Dear All,

I am new to SAP BTP and learning SAP Build Code. I am trying to create simple page where user can select Excel file and upload the data to back end table. At same time show uploaded data into frontend table.
Is this code generation possible using Joule ?
I am using trial version of SAP BTP

Regards,
Priyanka

entry.title: What are the BADI's available for Payment Medium Format in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Can you let me know what BADIs are available for Payment Medium Format and their function 

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: What are the BADI's available for Payment Medium Form...
entry.content:
See  

See help.sap.com documentation in relation to 

Configuring Payment Medium Formats

 

Here you will see link for 

Including Custom Reference Fields

See also blog https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/custom-logic-for-payment-files/ba-p/12828846

How to export a custom field (e-mail) in the payment file
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/how-to-export-a-custom-field-e-mail-in-the-payment-file/ba-p/13388363

See also help documentation Defining Custom Naming Rules for Payment Medium Files

Maintaining Format Mappings for Structured Addresses

entry.title: Re: Getting "Feature Disabled" status after the upgrade to Talent Intelligence Hub
entry.content:
Hi Ran,

Did you ever get this resolved? I'm running into the same issue as you when completing the TIH upgrade. 

Thanks, Lindsey

entry.title: Re: Maintenance order display blank screen in SAP Service and Asset Manager
entry.content: The issue is likely not with the App Update. A rule is failing when executing during the order detail navigation. You may have to attach debugger to check.

entry.title: For further credit and bank chain functionality difference ?
entry.content:
Hello All, 

Can anyone explain me 
1. Difference between Bank chain functionality and For Further credit FFC functionality.
2. Can we modify DMEE file after this get generated from SAP and before reaching to the bank, since there is a middleware that collects DMEE file and make additions or further instructions to the banks and sends the file to bank.  Hope this happens after file saved in AL11 directory. 

Please clarify me on this and with SAP KBA to follow procedure . 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Creating the Shell System for SAP Business Transformation Center: A Practical Approach
entry.content:
Hello @dfunkert 

Great blog, very well explained and detailled.

Just a minor question, what there a specific reason why you did not use DMO to HANA, instead of Heterogeneous DB Migration ?    Thanks

entry.title: ATC - How to Implement AUTHORITY-CHECK
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,

     I have implemented ATC in my company and I need to configure AUTHORITY-CHECK. From what I have seen, there is no standard AUTHORITY-CHECK. Has anyone implemented ATC with AUTHORITY-CHECK? Thank you very much in advance.

entry.title: Re: Bi weekly/Weekly Notifications for pending Time Sheet in SuccessFactors
entry.content: @NeeleshKamath , some how the custom MDF object I created is setting flag incorrectly. I reconfigured everything then it's working as expected. Thanks for response and advice.

entry.title: Re: Request to host a CodeJam in September 2025 (s'Hertogenbosch)
entry.content:
hi Eberechukwu,

I was wondering if any reach out has been done yet? If yes I might have missed it, can you let me know who have/will email me?

Kind regards,

HN

entry.title: 1H 2025 Release: Introducing SAP SuccessFactors Enterprise Service Management
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business environment, delivering exceptional employee experiences is not just nice to have—it is essential for organizational success. Traditional HR help desks and ticketing systems no longer meet the expectations of the modern workforce.

Introducing SAP SuccessFactors Enterprise Service Management, a comprehensive purpose-built HR Service Delivery solution designed to support how organizations deliver HR services. It provides HR service teams with AI-driven case management while empowering employees with intelligent, intuitive self-service tools to get quick answers and accelerate resolutions. By automating processes and using actionable insights to drive continuous improvements, organizations can improve employee satisfaction, boost HR service efficiency, and reduce HR services costs. 

Beyond traditional HR ticketing solution: Enhanced employee self-service experience

Today’s employees expect the same level of service they receive as consumers—available anytime, anywhere. SAP SuccessFactors Enterprise Service Management delivers just that, with seamless omnichannel access to HR services across SAP SuccessFactors solutions. Employees are empowered with self-service capabilities through quick actions, an integrated knowledge base, and guided experiences for all HR tasks.

Meet Joule, the AI copilot that seamlessly integrates proactive support into SAP SuccessFactors. As a virtual assistant for both employees and managers, Joule delivers intelligent, conversational support, allowing users to request information and complete HR tasks through natural language interactions. Joule’s capabilities extend beyond HR. It can be accessed across SAP applications, supporting a wide range of use cases and empowering users throughout the business with intelligent, self-service capabilities.

When HR support is needed, SAP SuccessFactors Enterprise Service Management removes the complexities of traditional ticketing and repetitive back-and-forth communications. Instead, requests begin with intuitive, step-by-step dynamic forms that personalize the experience, pre-populate relevant fields, and ensure precise input from the start.

The outcome? Employees gain confidence with requests being completed with fewer errors increasing employee satisfaction and engagement.

AI driven case management built for HR service rep: Boosting and scaling efficiency

 SAP SuccessFactors Enterprise Service Management offers AI-powered case management, specifically designed to meet HR's unique needs. Customizable dashboards and intelligent case prioritization allow HR professionals to focus on high-value tasks, providing exceptional service experiences. The solution’s intelligent case management ensures structured, compliant, and efficient HR operations, with personalized tools that speed up case resolution and enhance employee satisfaction.

Seamless integration with SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central allows HR teams to create cases effortlessly, pre-filling employee data and minimizing communication delays. Sensitive information, like payroll and performance data, remains securely within SAP’s trusted environment with role-based access. The 4i (4-eye) compliance check is integrated directly into the workflow, ensuring real-time visibility for HR agents and eliminating fragmented communication.AI capabilities are embedded throughout the service experience, including:

  • Interaction summaries that capture key conversation points

  • Resolution summaries for clear documentation

  • Automated email drafting to streamline communication

  • Content classification to accelerate case routing and resolution

The result?  HR service teams enjoy reduced manual effort, accelerated resolution times, and boost in productivity.

 Real-time analytics and low-code tools–Optimizing HR operations

Real-time analytics give HR leaders deep visibility into service performance, offering insights into case trends, resolution times, and SLA success rates—enabling continuous improvement and smarter strategic planning.

  • Real-time, actionable insights on case drivers, escalations and resolution times
  • Intuitive dashboards to track HR service rep performance and productivity over time
  • Custom reporting tailored to your organization’s specific needs

With low-code, personalized tools, organizations can easily tailor their HR service environment to meet specific business needs, without requiring deep technical expertise. The visual drag-and-drop interface allows HR teams to quickly build process-driven applications. This automation of key HR tasks—such as case management, ticket handling, and employee inquiries—leads to faster response times and less manual effort. By enabling real-time customization and automating workflows, organizations can improve agility and adapt to changing business needs. Additionally, the cost-effective nature of low-code tools reduces reliance on IT, allowing HR teams to manage and enhance their environment independently.

The result?  Organizations can uncover gaps, reduce cost and scale with confidence—without sacrificing quality.

The Bottom Line: HR Service Delivery Success

SAP SuccessFactors Enterprise Service Management represents a fundamental reimagining of HR service delivery. By combining powerful AI, thoughtful automation, and human-centered design, it creates an intelligent, proactive, and seamless experience for employees while empowering HR teams to work more efficiently.

Organizations that implement SAP SuccessFactors Enterprise Service Management can expect:

  • Higher employee satisfaction through faster, more personalized service
  • Reduced operational costs through automation and self-service
  • Increased HR team productivity and strategic impact
  • A unified solution that grows and adapts to changing workforce needs

In a world where employee experience directly impacts business outcomes, SAP SuccessFactors Enterprise Service Management provides the tools organizations need to deliver exceptional HR service at scale.

For a more comprehensive look at all our innovations, please visit newly launched SAP SuccessFactors Enterprise Service Management page on the SAP Help Portal.  For more solution info, please visit the SAP SuccessFactors Enterprise Service Management website. 

#SAP SuccessFactors Enterprise Service Management #HRServiceDelivery

entry.title: Re: Need to disable multiple users
entry.content: Actually no, and I may have been unclear. Of the 150+ user accounts, I need to permanently disable their ability to login. Either by changing the password, or by disabling the account. A handful (10 or so) people need to continue to be able to login as normal. It seems there ought to be a way to read a system table & issue an ALTER USER statement or similar for all users except the ones that need to be able to log in

entry.title: 503 erro on chat SAP AI Lauchpad
entry.content:
I'm experiencing an issue when using the chat in SAP AI Launchpad. I can make requests normally through the SAP AI Core API, but when I use the SAP AI Launchpad interface, I encounter the error shown in the image.

Is anyone else facing this? Does anyone have an idea how to solve this problem?

entry.title: CPI Connection Error
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am facing an issue, when we are trying to connect a URL using request reply and getting the data using JSON format as body and no authentication we re getting java.net.UnknownHostException:xxxxxxxxxx: Name or service not known

But when we are trying the same via postman/SOAP UI application we are getting successful response.

Can you please help us in resolving the issue

@Yee3 @Sriprasadsbhat

entry.title: Error in Outgoing Payments - Due Date Outside Permitted Range [Message 173-11]
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m encountering an error in Outgoing Payments, and the system throws the following message:

“The date falls outside the permitted range [Journal Entry - Due Date] [Message 173-11]”

I tried changing the due date in the posting periods thinking it would solve the issue, but the error still appears.

Could anyone please help me with this error?

Thank you!

 

Andersson U.

entry.title: Re: Successfactors LMS : Overdue learning notification
entry.content: @DivyaDokuru, different causes can lead to this situation. These are some points to be checked: (1) Is the email address part of another user profile in the same environment? | (2) Is the email coming from another environment, where the user has the email address set and notifications are active? | (3) Is the user a manager? The notification might be actually addressed to one of the subordinates (Creating Prompt for Users to Complete Courses That Are Near the Due Date | KBA 2642351). | (4) Is the notification customized? Sometimes, custom notifications are incorrect and the data comes mixed up. One user might see data in the notification that doesn't belong to her/his learning plan.

entry.title: Re: Getting Assertion failed error in RCM email template...
entry.content: One of my recruiters just got the same error too.

entry.title: Re: New member
entry.content:
Welcome @VitorMeireles ,

You can visit Porto Group, a Portuguese group in SAP Community.

We are preparing a big SAP Inside Track event in Porto at 12 SET 2025. Look here: https://community.sap.com/t5/porto/gh-p/porto

You can have more event information on our site: https://sitporto.com

entry.title: FSCM CM: Net Value of the itens from MM origin application
entry.content:
Hy gurus, 

I'd like to know if there is any customizing that I can update objects from MM with NET + TAX amount in FQM_FLOW table.  Nowadays I have only net amount in Cash Flow Analyzer.

Thanks in advance.

Best Regards

Att

RL

entry.title: SAP ABAP installation Error
entry.content:
Hello,

i am facing this error while trying to install SAP ABAP 7.52

**************************************************

Abort execution because of
Step returns modlib.jslib.caughtException

***************************************************

i searched for long time and tried many solution but i couldn't solve it

can any one help me please?
the log file of installation :
***************************************************

To install this TestDrive you have to accept
the SAP COMMUNITY DEVELOPER License (DEV).
Do you agree to the above license terms? yes/no:
User's response to the accept license prompt = 'y'
Please enter a password:
Please re-enter password for verification:
extracting data archives...
extracting /home/vhcalnplci/sapsetup/server/TAR/x86_64/sapmnt.tgz-*
Checking Linux kernel memory management parameters according to SAP Note 941735
Backing up /etc/sysctl.conf in /etc/sysctl.backup
Checking Linux kernel parameter vm.max_map_count according to SAP Note 900929
Checking presence of new SYBASE license files
Replaced the license file /sybase/NPL/SYSAM-2_0/licenses/SYBASE_ASE_TestDrive.lic
Install service
Check Installation Path ...

saphostexec is already running (pid=3844). Stopping...Stopped
ok
Copy Executable ...
ok
Generate Profile ...
ok
Install SYS-V service ...
ok
-> Start /usr/sap/hostctrl/exe/saphostexec pf=/usr/sap/hostctrl/exe/host_profile <-
start hostcontrol using profile /usr/sap/hostctrl/exe/host_profile
ok
[OK] SAPHostExec / SAPHostControl succesfully installed
Executing: "/usr/sap/hostctrl/exe/SAPCAR" -xvf "./SHADBMETRICSDEF.SAR" -R "/usr/sap/hostctrl/exe/config.d"
SAPCAR: processing archive ./SHADBMETRICSDEF.SAR (version 2.01)
x dbmetric.definition.db4.cimobj
x dbmetric.definition.db6.cimobj
x dbmetric.definition.hdb.cimobj
x dbmetric.definition.mss.cimobj
x dbmetric.definition.ora.cimobj
x dbmetric.definition.sap.cimobj
x dbmetric.definition.siq.cimobj
x dbmetric.definition.syb.cimobj
x dbmetric.definition.tst.cimobj
x dbmetrics.d
x dbmetrics.d/00.general.hdb.cimobj
x dbmetrics.d/00.state.hdb.cimobj
x dbmetrics.d/10.host.capacity.hdb.cimobj
x dbmetrics.d/20.indexserver.hdb.cimobj
x dbmetrics.d/20.nameserver.hdb.cimobj
SAPCAR: 15 file(s) extracted
SAPCAR: rc 0
=>sapparam(1c): No Profile used.
=>sapparam: SAPSYSTEMNAME neither in Profile nor in Commandline
################################################
Abort execution because of
Step returns modlib.jslib.caughtException
################################################

Error: sapinst has finished with an error code, please find logs in /tmp/sapinst_instdir
Error code: 20
++++ Support data section BEGIN ++++
* mount
/dev/sda on / type ext4 (rw,relatime)
devtmpfs on /dev type devtmpfs (rw,nosuid,size=4096k,nr_inodes=1013370,mode=755,inode64)
tmpfs on /dev/shm type tmpfs (rw,nosuid,nodev,inode64)
devpts on /dev/pts type devpts (rw,nosuid,noexec,relatime,gid=5,mode=620,ptmxmode=000)
sysfs on /sys type sysfs (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime)
securityfs on /sys/kernel/security type securityfs (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime)
cgroup2 on /sys/fs/cgroup type cgroup2 (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime,nsdelegate,memory_recursiveprot)
pstore on /sys/fs/pstore type pstore (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime)
bpf on /sys/fs/bpf type bpf (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime,mode=700)
proc on /proc type proc (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime)
tmpfs on /run type tmpfs (rw,nosuid,nodev,size=1627428k,nr_inodes=819200,mode=755,inode64)

hugetlbfs on /dev/hugepages type hugetlbfs (rw,nosuid,nodev,relatime,pagesize=2M)

configfs on /sys/kernel/config type configfs (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime)

gvfsd-fuse on /run/user/1000/gvfs type fuse.gvfsd-fuse (rw,nosuid,nodev,relatime,user_id=1000,group_id=100)

Filesystem Size Used Avail Use% Mounted on
/dev/sda 196G 56G 131G 30% /

/dev/sr0 52M 52M 0 100% /run/media/vhcalnplci/VBox_GAs_7.0.20

Filesystem Inodes IUsed IFree IUse% Mounted on
/dev/sda 13M 213K 13M 2% /
devtmpfs 990K 470 990K 1% /dev
tmpfs 994K 1 994K 1% /dev/shm
tmpfs 800K 788 800K 1% /run
tmpfs 1.0K 1 1023 1% /run/credentials/systemd-tmpfiles-setup-dev-early.service
tmpfs 1.0K 1 1023 1% /run/credentials/systemd-sysctl.service
tmpfs 1.0K 1 1023 1% /run/credentials/systemd-tmpfiles-setup-dev.service
tmpfs 1.0K 1 1023 1% /run/credentials/systemd-vconsole-setup.service
tmpfs 1.0K 1 1023 1% /run/credentials/systemd-tmpfiles-setup.service
ABAP_RAR_Sources 1000 -976K 977K - /media/sf_ABAP_RAR_Sources
tmpfs 199K 138 199K 1% /run/user/1000
tmpfs 1.0K 1 1023 1% /run/credentials/getty@tty1.service
/dev/sr0 0 0 0 - /run/media/vhcalnplci/VBox_GAs_7.0.20
* cat /etc/os-release
NAME="openSUSE Leap"
VERSION="15.6"
ID="opensuse-leap"
ID_LIKE="suse opensuse"
VERSION_ID="15.6"
PRETTY_NAME="openSUSE Leap 15.6"
ANSI_COLOR="0;32"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:opensuse:leap:15.6"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.opensuse.org"
HOME_URL="https://www.opensuse.org/"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://en.opensuse.org/Portal:Leap"
LOGO="distributor-logo-Leap"
* ls -ld / /sybase /usr/sap /sapmnt
drwxr-xr-x 25 root root 4096 May 23 11:18 /
drwxr-xr-x 3 root root 4096 Sep 6 2019 /sapmnt
drwxr-x--- 3 sybnpl sapsys 4096 Feb 20 2017 /sybase
drwxr-xr-x 7 root root 4096 May 23 12:32 /usr/sap
++++ Support data section END ++++
All logs have been written to the file /home/vhcalnplci/sapsetup/install_2025-05-23_14-14-32.log
Please see readme.html to find out how to get help

entry.title: SAP Communities: SAP Inside Track Porto 2025 web page
entry.content:
We have created a web page with all the information about the great SAP Inside Track Porto 2025 event, which we are organizing for September 27, 2025, in Vila Nova de Gaia.

The page brings together information about the event's location and how to get there using the various means of transport available, as well as who the sponsors are, who the organization team is, and who the volunteers are. In the future, we will post the lectures there.

We are also planning to have a SAPCodeJam the day before the event. You can find links to tickets, links to propose a lecture, and to be one of the speakers... The SAP community in Portugal is to be congratulated.

The page is wonderful, functional, and contextualized. What can I say about the Porto tiles matching the event image?

Great ideas happen when we are involved in a purpose. Register for the event and join the SAP Community in Portugal.

https://sitporto.com

entry.title: Re: Which function call returns 0? in sap abap
entry.content: Thanks @Sandra_Rossi for the comment, just added my examples.

entry.title: SAP Communities: SAP Inside Track Brazil page
entry.content:
The SAP Brazil community has created a page to publicize all SAP Inside Track Brazil events. For the year 2025, you can find the event schedule, links to tickets, and other information. Visit the SAP Brazil community page and stay updated with events in your city.

Visit: https://sitbrazil.com/

entry.title: Re: Nota Técnica 2021.003: Número de Protocolo para Nota Fiscal de Energia Elétrica (NF3e modelo 66)
entry.content:
Boa tarde Luize

Após aplicar as seguintes notas SAP (abaixo), 

3240161

1. Prerequisite Objects for NT 2021.003: 16-Digit Protocol Number for Energy NF3e - Part 1 

3240162

Prerequisite Objects for NT 2021.003: 16-Digit Protocol Number for Energy NF3e - Part 2 

3232974 

NT 2021.003: Enable 16-Digit Protocol Number for Energy NF3e 

3263831 

Incorrect Validation of the Protocol Number When Creating or Modifying an Electronic Nota Fiscal 

o campo Protocolo ficou disponivel corretamente:

Porém o protocolo não aparece na J1BNFE:

Isto está impedindo a validação da SEFAZ. 

Poderia me informar como corrigir este problema.

Obrigado

João Gaia

entry.title: Re: Actual Costs Not Reflected in Fiori (SAP CPM)
entry.content:
Do some research here on Forecasting: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/d6c1ceb7e0074cd1a8f28dad8a1a649c/1c16d11effd34cb78c6c0ab348a56f99.html

Dig into the forecasting workbook queries to see how the forecast is calculated.

If you still can't reconcile the values then please submit a SAP Support Ticket.

entry.title: sapcs?serverInfo
entry.content:
I am upgrading from Content Server 6.5 to Content Server 7.54 on our MaxDB database Windows 2016 server.  I am trying to create the new cs.conf file in the correct location, which I believe is C:\usr\sap\KP9\SYS\profile\cs.conf

The entire path was created by the Software Provisioning Tool, and I created the cs.conf file by copying the original ContentServer.ini file contents unchanged from the 6.5 Content Server

The serverInfo command that I am using is

http://<my_server_name>:1090/sapcs?serverInfo

 

403 - Forbidden: Access is denied

You do not have permission to view this directory or page using the credentials that you supplied.

 

I am an admin on the server.  I suspect that I do not have my cs.conf file set up correctly, or do not have it in the correct location.

This original command still works

http://<my_server_name>:1090/ContentServer/ContentServer.dll?serverInfo

entry.title: Re: System Replacement - Analysis for Microsoft Office
entry.content: We have several AO spreadsheets connected to our Neo SAC instance.  Those spreadsheets have been working fine for years.  We were recently forced to migrate from Neo to Cloud Foundry (CF).  SAP did the migration for us, and after I got our SAC data models connected to our new CF SAC instance, our reports within SAC are working fine.  However, I now need to redirect our AO spreadsheets from Neo to CF.  I would just like to make sure that what I am attempting to do can be done.  Can I change our existing AO spreadsheets so that they no longer point to our Neo SAC instance for data, but instead pull data from our CF SAC instance?  Or will I have to create new spreadsheets?  Thank you.

entry.title: Impact of Changing Cost Center on Settlement Rule in IW32 After Order Release
entry.content: In transaction IW32, once the order has been released and a settlement rule has already been assigned, my question is: if I change the cost center in the “Location” – “Account Assignment” view, should this change be reflected in the settlement rule?

entry.title: Re: Introducing the Background Processing Framework
entry.content: I dug around a bit in the packages and noticed two packages named private and public. Public contains the released objects while private seemed to have objects being developed for the future of the framework. I did see a bgmc multiple op process available with cl_bgmc_factory_ext class. Will this feature be released with 2025 ? Please would it be possible to explain some of the target use cases for this?

entry.title: Don't want approval Process for Receivables
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We do not want to allow an approval process for under/over payment customers.
Please let us know how we can disable the approval setting.

Thanks


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Don't want approval Process for Receivables
entry.content:
Dear All,

Review the "Clearing Strategies" activity and check if the "Create Manual Clearing Task If Overpaid" and/or "Create Manual Clearing Task If Underpaid" is enabled.

In case the Create Manual Clearing Task If Overpaid or Create Manual Clearing Task If Underpaid is enabled, the system will create a manual task to be approved.

Refer to the below documentation for additional details, please:

Configuration: Clearing Strategies - https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/0635ec3491974ad988be05d6b1dcf734/2d06d91d722d1014b2a1eb549940540c.html?locale=en-US

Also review the below KBA for further information:

KBA 2495072 - How to Set Up Approval Task for Under-/Overpayment https://me.sap.com/notes/2495072

Regards,
Anderson
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Need to disable multiple users
entry.content:
The SYSUSER table can be used for this. The challenge is how to identify the users that are to be disabled/deleted. The sample below is based on the demo database and three filters for users to process - one filter removes system users such as roles, another removes specific user types, and finally users that should be kept. You should consult a query that meets your specific needs and you should validate the users that returns in the result. 

begin -- create users for test...
    declare stmt long varchar;
    -- cleanup any test users
    select 
        list( string( 'drop user "', user_name , '"'), ';\n' ) into stmt
    from sysuser where user_name like 'u%';

    execute immediate stmt;    

    -- create test users
    select 
        list( string( 
           'create user "u', row_num, '" identified by sql' ), ';\n' )
        into stmt
    from sa_rowgenerator( 1, 100 );
    execute immediate stmt;
end;

-- this example disables users excluding system users, and
-- other identified users
begin
    declare stmt long varchar;
    -- disable users (by removing password)
    select 
        list(string(
          'alter user "',user_name,'" identified by NULL'),';\n')
    into stmt
    from sysuser 
    where   user_id < 1000000000        -- exclude roles
        and user_type not in(1,5,9,13)  -- filter user types
        and user_name not in            -- users to keep
          ( ('u20'),('u30'),('u40'),('u50'),('u60'),('u70')
           ,('DBA'),('GROUPO'),('BROWSER'),('Updater'),('ml_server'));
    execute immediate stmt;
end;

-- this example deletes users excluding system users, and
-- other identified users
begin
    declare stmt long varchar;
    -- disable users (by removing password)
    select 
        list(string(
          'drop user "',user_name,'"'),';\n')
    into stmt
    from sysuser 
    where   user_id < 1000000000       -- exclude roles
        and user_type not in(1,5,9,13) -- filter user types
        and user_name not in           -- users to keep
          ( ('u20'),('u30'),('u40'),('u50'),('u60'),('u70')
           ,('DBA'),('GROUPO'),('BROWSER'),('Updater'),('ml_server'));
    execute immediate stmt;
end;

entry.title: 91-club-invite-code-1627417228387
Re: SAP Screen Personas Global JavaScript Library for Similar Transactions
entry.content: Yes, you can do that; in fact, that was the way to use scripts across flavors before the introduction of global scripts as a separate object type. Uploading them as a resource and referencing them with session.utils.include still does work but it's just easier to handle such scenarios via global scripts.

entry.title: join-91-club-using-invite-code-1627417228387
verified-invite-code-91-club-1627417228387

Well Tamas, Am I doing something wrong? When I used the global script Id it raises an error message.

As you can see here is the global script:

entry.title: Re: Checkout cart returned seems to be randomly missing info that has been persisted on earlier chec
entry.content: Any solution on this issue ? Is it becuase of cookie issue ?

entry.title: Re: CMS SSL config and thick clients
entry.content:
I'm answering my own question to provide information on what I've found out.

I've done a lot of research and testing over the past few weeks, and also had an incident with SAP, and what I've learned is somewhat unsettling.

With the server and client both properly configured with CA-signed certificates, the only certificate validation that's done is:

  • Certificate effective/expiration date
  • Both server and client certs are trusted by the same root CA

Despite the documentation and SAP notes, the CN in each cert is completely ignored -- no hostname validation is performed by either side.  At least this is what I've found in all of my tests so far.

Distributing CA-signed certs to clients is unusual but not unheard of.  But in situations where they are, it's typically done for authentication -- the client authenticates to the server with a credential associated with the client cert.  But that's not happening here, so I'm struggling to understand why a client cert is needed at all.  But it is, so I need to configure it.

I also confirmed that once a client is configured for SSL, it can not connect to a non-SSL-enabled CMS.  I need to either run "sslconfig -protocol default" or toggle a registry key.  

Finally, I confirmed that although both the server and the client need to be pointed to five files (public cert, root cert, private key, passphrase, and pse), only the pse and passphrase files are actually used.  From what I can tell, the pse is a form of Java keystore and contains the two public certs and the private key.

/filesharepoc/Inbound/ReceiveFiles/${property.sourceFilename}

entry.title: Re: Fusion Forward: Unlocking Innovation with SAP Build – Series 1: What is Fusion Development?
entry.content: Completely boring

entry.title: Re: Offboarding 1.0 pending task concern when we are live...
entry.content:
Hi @singhvijendra ,

Yes, it is possible to have both Offboarding 1.0 (OFB 1.0) and Offboarding 2.0 (OFB 2.0) active in SAP SuccessFactors, but with important limitations and considerations.

Key Points to Consider
1. Coexistence of OFB 1.0 and OFB 2.0
SAP does not support full coexistence of Offboarding 1.0 and Offboarding 2.0 for the same population.

However, limited coexistence is possible if you segment the user groups clearly and apply targeted business rules.

Example: OFB 1.0 handles users already in offboarding before go-live of OFB 2.0.

OFB 2.0 handles all new offboarding cases post go-live.

2. Pending Offboarding Cases in OFB 1.0
You can keep Offboarding 1.0 active for pending cases, while new offboarding requests are handled by OFB 2.0.

Ensure you don’t deactivate OFB 1.0 immediately after go-live of OFB 2.0 to allow completion of in-progress cases.

3. System Configuration Requirements
You must ensure that Offboarding 2.0 business rules only trigger for new cases.

Use effective dating or eligibility rules to route employees to the appropriate version.

Communicate clearly with HR users to avoid manual triggering issues.

4. Data Migration
There is no migration path from OFB 1.0 to OFB 2.0 for in-process cases. These need to be completed in OFB 1.0.

Archive or document these cases if needed for compliance before final deactivation of OFB 1.0.

Recommended Guidance
1. Review all in-flight offboarding cases in OFB 1.0 and estimate completion time.
2. Configure eligibility rules to divert new offboardings to OFB 2.0 only.
3. Keep OFB 1.0 active for legacy/pending cases until all are completed.
4. Monitor both workflows for issues or overlap.
5. After all OFB 1.0 cases are closed, deactivate Offboarding 1.0.

Hope this helps!! Kindly let me know if any queries.

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Re: Export Data to Excel in SAP Build Apps Using JavaScript and Flow Functions
entry.content:

entry.title: TPM44 acrrual interest calculation
entry.content:
With standard method in while calculating accrual system on last day of month post accrual and reverse next day and following month it cumulate with previous period interest 

Is there a way of posting accrued interest and will not reverse on next day but will clear all accrued income at the end of the term with offsetting entry with incoming payment.

Kindly reply stuck in this

entry.title: Re: Unloading data from a SAP Build Apps VCF backend to CSV files
entry.content:
Hi @v_blum2000 

Let add BOM = '\uFEFF' as below:

const BOM = '\uFEFF';
const csv = BOM + csvBody;

Regards,

Dung Vo

entry.title: Re: Impact of Changing Cost Center on Settlement Rule in...
entry.content:
Hi,

Generally the cost center in location/account assignment, if configured, is used in the settlement rule as cost receiver. However, the settlement rule is not updated automatically if there are changes in location/account assignment.

Regards,

Vijay

entry.title: Re: SAP Experts: Are We Still Building for the Past, or Architecting for the Clean Core Future?
entry.content:
Dear Member

how this post is helping to resolve  a probekk

entry.title: Hi Narendra,I have the same requirement but not found any solution. Could you please suggest?
entry.content:
Hi All,

I want an example showing how to add steps to a Job using FM JOB_SUBMIT.

Requirement goes as 3 dependent jobs should run one after another only after the previous job is finished and I want to add them as steps in one job and execute it through an ABAP report.

How can this be achieve? how many JOB_CLOSE and JOB_SUBMIT FMs will be required and in what ordered?

Note : It is must that this should be achieved through a report and not using SM36.

Thanks in advance!

Regards,
Pooja

entry.title: Scheduling background job through ABAP program which us dependent on first job completion
entry.content:
Requirement goes as 3 dependent jobs should run one after another only after the previous job is finished and I want to add them as steps in one job and execute it through an ABAP report.

How can this be achieve? how many JOB_CLOSE and JOB_SUBMIT FMs will be required and in what ordered?

Tried the below approach

For a chain of 3 jobs:

  1. Create job 1 but don't call JOB_CLOSE
  2. Create job 2 with preceding job = job 1
  3. Create job 3 with preceding job = job 2
  4. Define the start condition of job 1 = call JOB_CLOSE
    Note: JOB_CLOSE with start condition = preceding job Job1 (parameters PRED_JOBCOUNT and PRED_JOBNAME)

    Whatever I am doing is not working and leaving all 3 jobs in Scheduled status and not released one after another one the previous Job is completed.

    Also, I already went through documentation couldn't find any proper example that support my requirement. Help me if you have any idea regarding the same.

entry.title: Re: Export Data to Excel in SAP Build Apps Using JavaScript and Flow Functions
entry.content:
Hi @DungVT37 

Thank you for going through the blog and trying out the solution!

The encoding issue you're encountering with special characters like ä, ö, or ü usually occurs when the base64 string isn't correctly preserving UTF-8 encoding before being converted into a Blob and downloaded.

To assist you better, could you please share:

  • The base64 string you're receiving (or a sample of it)?

  • The original Excel file you expect to download?

With these, I can reproduce the issue and provide a more accurate solution or adjustment.

Looking forward to your response!

entry.title: Re: Support Dashboard weiteren Mitarbeiter auf eigene Anf...
entry.content:
Hi martina_93, 

To access SAP for Me, an S-user needs to have the authorization to "Display all Cases" in order to view cases created by other S-users.

For more detailed information, please refer to the KBAs and community below:
How do I get access to SAP for Me?
1812813 - How to view or search for cases - SAP for Me

If you have any questions, please open a case under the component XX-SER-FORME using Get Support (Cases, Expert Chat, ...)

Best regards,
Judy

entry.title: SAP SF EC Associations
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP SF EC, after creating an association in the child object, why do we still need to configure field criteria? When we create an association, it should filter the values in the child object based on the value selected in the parent object, right? Then why do we need to configure field criteria again in Manage Business Configuration (MBC)?

entry.title: Hi, we have a Classic opex model with legal entity, profit center, cost center and wbs.
entry.content:
We need to validate input at cost center level by controlling legal entity and profit center with a validation based in cost center and in parallel to validate input at wbs level by controlling legal entity and profit center with a validation based on wbs. Let’s say: respecting classi sap data model. The problem is that sac do not accept two rules to validate legal entity and profit center . How can sac support opex planning avoiding user to input wrong combinations? Create dedicated separated models is not one Solution because we need total opex in one single place.

thanks

stefano

entry.title: Re: Financial Planning: Validation Rules blocking input for unassigned member
entry.content: We need to validate input at cost center level by controlling legal entity and profit center with a validation based in cost center and in parallel to validate input at wbs level by controlling legal entity and profit center with a validation based on wbs. Let’s say: respecting classi sap data model. The problem is that sac do not accept two rules to validate legal entity and profit center . How can sac support opex planning avoiding user to input wrong combinations? Create dedicated separated models is not one Solution because we need total opex in one single place. thanks stefano

entry.title: Re: FSCM CM: Net Value of the itens from MM origin applic...
entry.content: Only Non deductible input tax (EKPO-NAVNW) will be calculated in the MM forecasted amount. Deductible taxes are not included.

Am creating a JSP page in SAP MII which is using a custom jar file. How can I upload the jar file in SAP MII to get the reference in JSP page.

I have tried the following but no luck.

1) Uploaded jar files in the same way we upload custom actions blocks.

2)Uploaded jar file in web folder 

currently I am getting error while jsp compilation due to missing class file.

Am on SAP MII 15.1 SP5

 

 

Any help on this very much appreciated.

 

Thanks

Shaji

entry.title: Re: Upload payroll file into MultiBank Connectivity Monitor
entry.content: SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity is integrated with payroll processes. For further details, kindly refer to the SAP Help Portal SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity

entry.title: Re: Ui5 init command not working
entry.content: Hi install using this command in CLI = > npm install --global @ui5/cli

entry.title: Payment Amount Greater Than Invoice Amount \[VPM2.SumApplied] - CCO Monitor
entry.content:
Description:
We're using *SAP Customer Checkout (CCO) FP21, integrated with **SAP Business One*, and encountering the following error when settling an invoice:

> [VPM2.SumApplied] Payment amount is greater than invoice amount

Scenario:

* Invoice currency: AED (local currency)
* Payment currency: USD (foreign currency)
* Invoice is fetched from SAP B1 into CCO
* Customer pays in USD (e.g., \$28)
* Exchange rate (USD → AED): e.g., 1 USD = 3.67
* This converts to AED 102.76
* Invoice total is only AED 100
* CCO displays the change (2.76 AED) correctly
* But when syncing to SAP B1, it throws the error:
Payment amount is greater than invoice amount [VPM2.SumApplied]

Suspected Cause:

Although change is correctly returned to the customer in CCO, the full amount (102.76 AED) is still being sent to SAP B1 as SumApplied, instead of just the applied invoice amount (100 AED).

Question:

How can we configure SAP CCO to only apply the exact invoice amount (100 AED) when the payment is made in a foreign currency (USD), and handle the rest as change — without triggering this error in SAP B1?

entry.title: import outbound delivery through the Excel
entry.content:
Hi ,

I create a sales order , and  I should create the outbound delivery base on the sales order through the APP Create Outbound Delivery with Order Reference.

But my question is : the sales order have hundreds of lines , I just need part of it . If in APP Create Outbound Delivery with Order Reference , I have to select in the SAP but cannot set flexible combinations of filtering criteria . This leads to low work efficiency .

SO I want to download the sales order to the Excel , select in the Excel , and import outbound delivery through the Excel . Is there a way ?

entry.title: Production Order - Product Cost by Order (End to End Flow with WIP and Variance)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Consultants,

In this blog, I have given detailed explanation of end to end flow of Production order ( Product Cost By order) with WIP and Variance. This blog will give you better idea about when WIP and Variance is calculated and posted. This will help you when business asks you to provide the details about WIP and Variance details related to Production Order.

Detailed explanation of Production Order – Product Cost by Order end to end flow with accounting entries in SAP.

Product Cost by Order is a cost accounting method in SAP used to calculate and track the costs related to production orders. It accumulates all actual costs incurred during production (materials, labor, overheads) on a specific production order.

 

Creation of Production Order

  • Process: A production order is created to manufacture a product.

Note - No accounting entry is posted at this stage.

 

Release of Production Order

  • The order is released to begin execution. Reservations for materials are triggered.

Note - No accounting entry is posted at this stage.

 

Goods Issue to Production Order

  • Raw materials are issued from inventory to the production order for manufacturing.
  • Accounting Entries:
    • Debit: Raw Consumption Account (e.g., Raw Material Consumption A/c) – GBB/VBR in OBYC.
    • Credit: Material Stock Account (Raw Material Inventory A/c) – BSX in OBYC.

 

Goods Receipt from Production Order

  • Finished or semi-finished goods are received into inventory after production is complete.
  • Accounting Entries:
    • Debit: Material Stock Account (Finished/Semi-Finished Goods Inventory A/c) – BSX in OBYC.
    • Credit: COGM (Offsetting Entry, e.g., Cost of Goods Produced A/c) – GBB/AUF in OBYC.

 

 

WIP vs. Variance Calculation: Understanding the Basics

In case of Product Cost by Order, system can calculate either WIP (Work in Progress) or Variance, but never both simultaneously for a single order. The calculation depends on the status of the production order:

  • Order Status Not TECO or DEL:
    Only WIP is calculated but actual posting to Finance will be done Order settlement is done at the month end.
  • Order Status Changed to TECO (Technically Complete) or DEL (Delivered):
    WIP is reversed, and Variance is calculated but FI posting will be done at the time of settlement during month end.

 

How Does the System Calculate WIP and Variance?

The key formula used for calculation is:

GR Value - (GI Value + Activity Value + Overheads)

Where:

  • GR (Goods Receipt) Value: Value of materials received against the order.
  • GI (Goods Issue) Value: Value of goods issued.
  • Activity Value: Cost of activities performed.
  • Overheads: Allocated overhead costs.
  • WIP represents the value of production that is still in progress.
  • Variance represents the difference between actual debits and credits on the order.

 

 

Accounting Entries in Production Order for WIP and Variance

 

  1. WIP Entries (During Production)

At the first settlement stage, when the order is still in progress, the accounting entry typically is:

  • Dr: WIP Inventory Account (Balance Sheet)
  • Cr: Change in WIP Account (Profit & Loss)

 

  1. Variance Entries (After Order Completion)

Once the order is Technically Complete (TECO) or Delivered (DLV), variance is calculated and settled with two accounting documents. WIP will be reversed and Variance document will be posted.

WIP reversal accounting document:

  • Dr: Change in WIP Account
  • Cr: WIP Inventory Account

Variance accounting document:

Case 1- Actual cost is less than the planned cost.

  • Dr: Cost of Goods Manufactured (COGM) Account
  • Cr: Production Variance Account

Case 2 – Actual cost is more than the planned cost.

  • Dr: Production Variance Account
  • Cr: Cost of Goods Manufactured (COGM) Account

 

Note: Both debit and credit for variance postings are in the Profit & Loss account, which means variance does not affect overall profitability directly.

 

Important Clarifications

  • WIP or Variance depends solely on order status, unlike product cost by period, where both can occur in a product cost collector.
  • WIP account should be zero. If it is not, this means production is still ongoing, and the stock is still at the work-in-progress stage.
  • Target Cost is calculated as the goods received from the production order quantity multiplied by the material standard price. It used as a basis for variance calculation.

Understanding Cost Types in Production Orders

Cost Type

Formula or Explanation

Target Cost

GR Quantity × Material Standard Price

Plan Price

Planned Order Quantity × Planned Price

Actual Cost

Actual Quantity × Actual Price (available post month-end)

Net Actual Cost

Total Actual Cost – WIP

 

Production Order Settlement Process

Settlement ensures all costs are properly accounted for and the order balance reaches zero. This includes:

  • Confirming the order (Status: CNF)
  • Posting Goods Receipt (GR) against the order (Status: DLV)
  • Marking the order as Technically Complete (Status: TECO) if no jobs are pending

 

I hope this blog will help you to understand the Production order ( Product Cost by Order) process and how WIP and Variance is calculated and posted. Please provide your feedback.

Thank you

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Re: sap s4hana cloud - creating journal entry with Busine...
entry.content:
Hi,

Requesting you to post full details of the issue. Pls share what error you are getting and what entry you are posting. Clear details will help others to provide solutions.

 

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP With Prostgres Serice in BTP
entry.content:
@satya-dev @Santosh_RG ,

i was able to fix the issue, you should add the sslcert and the sslrootcert as well also increase the timeout to give time to the connection.

 

      "db": {
        "kind": "postgres",
        "credentials": {
          "hostname": "127.0.0.1",
          "port": "12345",
          "username": "...",
          "password": "...",
          "dbname": "...",
          "sslcert": "-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----\n...\n-----END CERTIFICATE-----",
          "sslrootcert": "-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----\n...\n-----END CERTIFICATE-----"
        },
        "pool": {
          "acquireTimeoutMillis": 2000
        }
      }

entry.title: Re: Issue with connection to postgresql from SAP CAP app...
entry.content:
The solution is too add the sslcert and sslrootcert and increase the timeout.

Example: 

      "db": {
        "kind": "postgres",
        "credentials": {
          "hostname": "127.0.0.1",
          "port": "12345",
          "username": "...",
          "password": "...",
          "dbname": "...",
          "sslcert": "-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----\n...\n-----END CERTIFICATE-----",
          "sslrootcert": "-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----\n...\n-----END CERTIFICATE-----"
        },
        "pool": {
          "acquireTimeoutMillis": 2000
        }
      }

entry.title: Re: Retention Amount in Sales Order Showing with VAT in Manage Customer Line item– Require Exclusion

entry.title: Re: How to reverse COP1/COP2 created HU for the Process O...
entry.content: But, that will mean to delete/cancel the full material document first, and then to post it again.
